items.title: Mouse plague so bad locals say driving feels like 'popping bubble wrap'

children.content: As locals in a Western Australian town battle a siege of mice, farmers still do not have permission to use a high-strength bait to combat them.

gallery.altText: A close up of a dozen dead mice on a shovel, Robert is wearing blue pants in the background.

thumbnail.altText: A close up of a dozen dead mice on a shovel, Robert is wearing blue pants in the background.

items.title: Passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship to arrive in Perth tomorrow morning

children.content: Six passengers from a cruise ship at the centre of a deadly hantavirus outbreak are testing negative for the illness and are symptom-free, the federal health minister says, as they prepare to be repatriated to Australia.

image.altText: A plane on the tarmac at the airport surrounded by people in white body suits carrying white bags.

items.title: Police investigate suspected homicide under 'cover of darkness' in WA farming town

children.content: The man was found unresponsive on a suburban street in Katanning early yesterday morning.

image.altText: police on a street

items.title: Green light for Andrew Forrest's $70m Ningaloo Reef resort

children.content: After almost a decade of planning, mining billionaire Andrew Forrest's plan to build an eco-resort on the shores of the Ningaloo Reef has received the go-ahead.

gallery.altText: A mountain covered in shrubs and five cabins with balconies lined up next to each other
Landscape picture showing clouds, ocean and shrub bushland
An artist impression showing a pool with mountain in the background

thumbnail.altText: A mountain covered in shrubs and five cabins with balconies lined up next to each other
Landscape picture showing clouds, ocean and shrub bushland
An artist impression showing a pool with mountain in the background

items.title: Residents worried about emergency access after bridge closes

children.content: The closure of a bridge linking Wheatbelt residents to their town sparks concern about emergency response times and access to services, including disability support.

image.altText: A woman wearing glasses and a beaning looks at the camera.

items.title: Lithium giant offers free car wash for residents battling dust from its mine

children.content: Residents plagued by dust from Australia's oldest and largest lithium mine say a plan for a free car wash by the mine's owner misses the mark.

image.altText: An aerial shot of the Greenbushes mining pits.

items.title: Record native title compensation decision hailed as a 'watershed case'

children.content: Andrew Forrest's Fortescue has been ordered to hand over the largest native title compensation payout in Australian history. But some say it falls painfully short of what is owed.

articles.title: Record payout after billionaire built mine without traditional owners' permission

children.content: Andrew Forrest's Fortescue has been ordered to pay $150 million to WA traditional owners after mining their land without permission.

image.altText: A composite image of Andrew Forrest (left) and Michael Woodley situated at a Fortescue mine in WA.

gallery.altText: Michael Woodley speaks to reporters outside court.

thumbnail.altText: Michael Woodley speaks to reporters outside court.

items.title: Man facing drug charges after woman's body found outside cafe in Joondalup

children.content: Police are investigating after a woman's body was discovered on Grand Boulevard in Joondalup, with a 35-year-old man charged with a string of drug offences.

image.altText: A note on the cafe window where the woman's body was found.

items.title: Inquest finds treatment of prisoner in lead up to death 'reprehensible'

children.content: Corey Desmond Devree died when he was left alone for extended periods of time at the prison's paint workshop.

image.altText: The front gate of a medium security prison.

items.title: Coles found to have misled shoppers in bombshell Federal Court case

children.content: Supermarket giant Coles broke consumer law by misleading shoppers on discount prices, a Federal Court judge has found.

image.altText: A close up of a red sign with Coles spelt out in white letters.

items.title: These charts show how Coles Down Down pricing patterns duped shoppers

children.content: The pricing patterns of these six products show how Coles was found to have misled consumers through its Down Down pricing patterns.

image.altText: A illustration mockup of a chart showing the Coles white label price vs the Down Down price.

items.title: Ex-bikie boss who paid sniper to kill rival gets 34 years in jail

children.content: David Pye, who was convicted last year of hiring a sniper to assassinate his rival Nick Martin during a drag racing event, was sentenced to 34 years in jail.

gallery.altText: A man named David Pye has tattoos and smiles as he walks down stairs.

thumbnail.altText: A man named David Pye has tattoos and smiles as he walks down stairs.

items.title: Power to cut funding and stricter 'permanence' test under new NDIS

children.content: Legislation designed to save $35 billion in NDIS spending is introduced to parliament, revealing new powers for the minister to cut funding to classes of supports, automate some decisions and create requirements for prospective participants to exhaust all other treatment options before entering the NDIS.

image.altText: A close-up profile image of a man with short hair, wearing a suit. The background is dark.

items.title: Prime minister concedes aged care wait times 'not good enough'

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has conceded the state of aged care in Australia "is not good enough" after a new report revealed people are waiting an average of a year for services.

image.altText: Albanese looking down in a suit while touching his ear.

items.title: Terror accused charged with child abuse, extremist material offences

children.content: A man accused of planning terrorist attacks in Perth is charged with further offences, including possession of child abuse material and violent extremist material on his devices.

image.altText: A man smiles at a camera with a digger behind him

items.title: Homebuilding reforms to let WA customers walk away from failing builders sooner

children.content: New legislation introduced to state parliament aim to prevent Western Australians from having to suffer the financial and emotional strain of not having a home if their building company collapses.

image.altText: A couple inspects their unfinished Nicheliving home.

items.title: Sexual abuse, a thwarted Olympic dream, and the sister who uncovered the truth

children.content: Ian Walker was one of the most promising athletes in the country until his life started to unravel, and the search for the truth in his tragic story has finally come to light.

gallery.altText: An older image of three young siblings arm in arm smiling at each other.
A young man wearing black pants and a blue jumper stands smiling beneath a poster of Jim Morrison.

thumbnail.altText: An older image of three young siblings arm in arm smiling at each other.
A young man wearing black pants and a blue jumper stands smiling beneath a poster of Jim Morrison.

items.title: E-scooter rider dies after being hit by car while crossing Perth highway

children.content: A man in his 40s is struck by a hatchback and killed while riding an e-scooter across Reid Highway in Perth's northern suburbs.

image.altText: A generic photograph of an unidentified WA Police officer wearing a high visibility police vest over a blue uniform.

items.title: Email reveals Jewish group warned police about Bondi gunman in 2019

children.content: A Jewish security agency raised concerns about Bondi Beach gunman Naveed Akram years before the terror attack that left 15 people dead, a document shows.

image.altText: Naveed Akram

items.title: Women's refuge turns to smarter solutions to fight tracking technology

children.content: A women's shelter in regional Western Australia has had to adapt to an increase in technology-facilitated violence to keep victims safe.

image.altText: Woman holding an iphone

items.title: Labor's 'jaw dropping' fracking decision wedges One Nation at first opportunity

children.content: The Malinauskas government's push to end the fracking moratorium in South East SA is a brutal welcome to politics for a new One Nation MP, as Labor fights an environmental war against both the left and right.

image.altText: Two side-by-side photos of two different men

items.title: Solar eclipse to attract thousands to 'middle of nowhere' in 2028

children.content: One of the longest solar eclipses this century, albeit for five minutes, is set to put the remote East Kimberley on the world map.

gallery.altText: A child wearing eclipse glasses looks up at the sky
blonde woman with blue top posing next to an information sign

thumbnail.altText: A child wearing eclipse glasses looks up at the sky
Artemis II — a sunrise for orion
blonde woman with blue top posing next to an information sign

items.title: Taser-death inquest told officers had 'plenty of time' to deal with 95yo

children.content: A senior NSW Ambulance officer tells the Clare Nowland inquest that emergency services could have taken more time responding to the 95-year-old's dementia-related aggression.

image.altText: an elderly woman looking at the camera

items.title: Voss exit shuts door on Carlton's premiership era that never was

children.content: Michael Voss leaves Carlton having taken the club to places it has rarely been in a quarter of a century, but still adrift from where it ultimately wants to be.

image.altText: Michael Voss with both fists raised in celebration

items.title: Entire university sector accused of antisemitism failure

children.content: The special envoy on antisemitism has found not a single Australian university has met its requirements for adopting a definition of antisemitism.

image.altText: Great Court walkway at the University of Queensland.

items.title: Non-citizens to be banned from NDIS under Coalition policy

children.content: Non-citizens, including permanent residents, will be barred from accessing welfare under an updated migration crackdown promised by the Coalition.

image.altText: Profile of a man.

items.title: Father allegedly high on 'nangs' in crash that killed daughters, as scuffle erupts outside court

children.content: A Perth father was allegedly high on 'nangs' during a crash that killed his two daughters, as a scuffle with media erupts at court.

A section of road with some trees alongside.
